title: GET exposes admin escalation data without server-side authorization

evidence.quote: Protected: only callable from the /admin page (owner-gated in UI)

reasoning: The route is documented as protected and the POST handler enforces x-admin-secret, but GET returns all unresolved escalation records without checking any credential. UI owner-gating does not protect the API route, so any network caller who can reach /api/admin/escalations can enumerate pending dispute escalation data.
reproduction: Send GET /api/admin/escalations without x-admin-secret; the handler reads escalations.json and returns { escalations, total } instead of 403.
recommendation: Apply the same server-side admin authentication check to GET as POST, ideally via a shared helper used by both handlers. If browser access is required, use a server-validated session/cookie rather than relying on the admin page UI.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are provided for this route, and there is no test asserting unauthenticated GET requests are rejected.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add route handler tests that call GET without credentials and with an invalid x-admin-secret and assert 403, then call with a valid credential and assert pending escalations are returned.
minimumFixScope: Update frontend/src/app/api/admin/escalations/route.ts to enforce server-side admin authorization in GET and cover it with a focused route test.
